{"key_event":"New Policies and provincial assemblies","reasoning":"Late Qing reforms modestly rebuilt administrative, police, educational, and military capacity after the Boxer disaster, but foreign constraints, indemnities, provincial militarization, and a weak regency kept the central state weak. Civil society rose substantially as modern schools, press, chambers of commerce, boycotts, constitutionalist groups, revolutionary networks, and 1909 provincial assemblies gave urban and provincial elites durable platforms for mobilization.","society_change":1.3,"society_power":5.8,"state_change":0.2,"state_power":4.2}
